items.builder_takeaway: Concrete reference design for agentic incident response with a real approval/permission model.
source_facts: Vercel Agent now offers dashboard chat, production investigations, and approval-gated remediation actions in public beta for Pro/Enterprise teams.
items.title: Vercel Agent expands to production investigations and approved actions (public beta)
items.uncertainty: Beta scope and quality of the 'approved actions' remediation flow unverified beyond vendor description.

items.builder_takeaway: Concrete cost figure for cheap, fast image generation - useful if budgeting an image-gen feature.
source_facts: Nano Banana 2 Lite (gemini-3.1-flash-lite-image) generates 1K images at $0.034 each via Vercel AI Gateway.
items.title: Nano Banana 2 Lite image model lands on Vercel AI Gateway at $0.034/image
items.uncertainty: Image quality/consistency vs full Nano Banana 2 not independently verified here.

items.builder_takeaway: Relevant if evaluating hosted sandboxes for agent code execution.
source_facts: Vercel Sandbox now supports launching from custom OCI images while preserving Fluid Compute cold-start performance.
items.title: Vercel Sandbox supports custom root filesystem images
items.uncertainty: Cold-start performance claim with custom images not independently benchmarked.

items.builder_takeaway: Vercel now hosts an OCI-compliant container registry with standard docker push/pull/tag workflows and unlimited repos per project. Mainly plumbing for the new Sandbox custom images and Dockerfile-based Functions features.
source_facts: Vercel Container Registry (VCR) is a new OCI-compliant image registry integrated with standard Docker tooling.
items.title: Vercel launches its own OCI container registry (VCR)
items.uncertainty: Pricing for storage/egress not stated in the changelog excerpt.

items.builder_takeaway: Vercel now lets you deploy multiple frontends/backends as 'Services' within one project, sharing a domain and talking to each other privately without going over the public internet, with coordinated build/preview/rollback.
source_facts: Vercel Services allows deploying multiple frameworks/services within a single project with private internal networking.
items.title: Vercel Services: multiple frameworks in one project
items.uncertainty: Unclear how this compares cost/complexity-wise to just running separate projects.

items.builder_takeaway: Meaningfully widens what can run on Vercel's serverless platform - relevant if you were avoiding Vercel for non-JS/Python workloads.
source_facts: Vercel Functions now support deploying OCI/Dockerfile-based HTTP servers on Fluid compute, removing prior language/runtime restrictions.
items.title: Vercel Functions now run arbitrary Dockerfiles on Fluid Compute
items.uncertainty: Cold-start and pricing behavior for arbitrary containers vs native runtimes not detailed here.

items.builder_takeaway: The world runs on open source software. The frameworks, libraries, and tools we rely on are made possible by communities that share ideas and build in the open. At Vercel, we want to help those communities thrive. That’s why we run the : a…
source_facts: Vercel Open Source Program: Spring 2026 cohort
items.title: Vercel Open Source Program: Spring 2026 cohort

items.builder_takeaway: Regulatory change directly affecting model availability for Claude users.
source_facts: The US Department of Commerce lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, and Anthropic is restoring access starting the following day.
items.title: US Commerce Dept lifts export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.uncertainty: Full scope of which regions/accounts regain access, and timeline, not yet detailed beyond the tweet.

items.builder_takeaway: Hands-on test of the new cheap/fast Gemini image model; anecdotal quality impressions only.
source_facts: Nano Banana 2 Lite is Google's fastest and cheapest Gemini image model, tested informally by Simon Willison.
items.title: Simon Willison tries Nano Banana 2 Lite

items.builder_takeaway: Direct read on whether to move workloads to Sonnet 5 for cost savings, from a trusted, hands-on source.
source_facts: Claude Sonnet 5 offers performance close to Opus 4.8 at a lower price point, with intentionally limited cyber capability versus Mythos 5.
items.title: What's new in Claude Sonnet 5
items.uncertainty: Benchmark specifics behind 'close to Opus 4.8' not independently verified.

items.builder_takeaway: A political-compass-style quiz sorting people into 30 AI-ethics archetypes; notable only for being a no-build single-page React app.
source_facts: The AI Compass is a 29-question quiz that categorizes respondents into AI-ethics archetypes.
items.title: The AI Compass quiz

items.builder_takeaway: Reusable, open-source technique for having your own coding agents produce verifiable video evidence of changes.
source_facts: shot-scraper 1.10 adds a 'video' command that records a Playwright-driven video from a storyboard.yml script, letting coding agents generate demo videos of their own work.
items.title: shot-scraper video: agents can now record demos of their own work

items.builder_takeaway: Release announcement pointing to the 'video' command feature; no additional information beyond the companion post.
source_facts: shot-scraper 1.10 was released, headlined by the new 'video' storyboard command.
items.title: shot-scraper 1.10 release notes

items.builder_takeaway: Credible, reproducible trust/transparency issue in a tool many builders (including this pipeline's operator) use daily.
source_facts: Claude Code silently embeds hidden Unicode markers and altered date formatting in its system prompt when routed through certain non-default API endpoints, discovered via binary reverse-engineering.
items.title: Claude Code caught steganographically marking requests based on hidden domain/timezone list
items.uncertainty: No official Anthropic statement/confirmation yet found; based on independent reverse engineering, though corroborated by multiple outlets.

items.title: Anthropic launches Claude Science, an AI workbench for researchers
